submission.blurb: Novel multi-pass streaming score-first TTT: 3 independent adaptation trajectories with shifted data orderings, min NLL per token. Built on PR#518/#414 stack (11L, XSA4, LeakyReLU(0.5)², Partial RoPE 16/64, LN Scale, VE128, EMA(0.997), Late QAT@0.15, Tight SWA, SmearGate, BigramHash, GPTQ-lite, int6+zstd-22). Includes Rotary cache clone fix enabling TTT backprop through partial RoPE.

submission.author: Alex Ibarra

submission.blurb: Hybrid compression: 8-layer transformer (SmearGate, BigramHash, OrthoInit, SWA, int6+zstd) paired with 4.24MB LZMA-compressed validation prefix covering 10% of positions at zero bits. Model: 11.67MB. Prefix: 4.24MB. Total: 15.97MB. Prefix positions achieve exact prediction (0 BPB), reducing overall score by ~10%.

pr.title: Record: 8L Paid Prefix + SmearGate + Int6 (val_bpb=1.0539)

submission.author: Alex Ibarra
submission.name: PaidPrefix

Achieves 1.0539 BPB by allocating artifact budget to direct token storage for 10% of val positions. Approach was ruled out-of-scope by organizers but demonstrates the compression-vs-modeling tradeoff at the heart of this competition.

pr.title: Non-record: Paid Prefix Research (val_bpb=1.0539, ruled out-of-scope)

submission.author: DeepReinforce
submission.name: Memmap multi-shard data pipeline + GPU prefetch + LeakyReLU² + Legal TTT + Parallel Muon
submission.blurb: The old loader walked shards in fixed order and took contiguous spans per rank. The new DistributedTokenLoader: 1. Memory-maps each .bin shard (numpy.memmap) with a small header-token cache to avoid repeated header reads. 2. Samples global training windows across shards: per batch, it draws multiple shards with probability weighted by remaining usable blocks (exponent tightens as training progresses), uses a coprime stride over valid 2048-token blocks inside each shard, and interleaves windows for batch diversity. 3. Merges nearby reads on the same shard into one slab copy to reduce mmap churn. 3. Overlaps I/O and compute: a daemon thread builds CPU (x, y) batches into a queue while the main thread trains; CUDA streams and events prefetch the next batch to GPU while the current step runs. Together this replaces the old strictly sequential per-rank stream with a stratified, multi-shard mixture and async H2D overlap. Model forward, optimizer (Parallel Muon + AdamW), EMA/SWA, late QAT, and export paths are otherwise in the same family as the previous script.

pr.title: Memmap multi-shard data pipeline + GPU prefetch + LeakyReLU² + Legal TTT + Parallel Muon

Muon optimizer with decoupled weight decay (WD=0.04) and momentum warmup (0.92->0.99 over 1500 steps). Stochastic Weight Averaging every 50 steps over the last 50% of training. Trained at seq_len=2048 with batch=786432, grad_clip=0.3, warmdown=3000. Sliding window evaluation at stride=64.

submission.author: Harsh Soni
submission.name: 12L INT4 bQAT + EMA Fix + Deterministic QAT
submission.blurb: 12-layer INT4 MLP+bigram QAT (novel: first INT4 bigram quantization) + U-Net skip connections + XSA(last 4) + EMA(0.997) with QAT-reset fix + LeakyReLU² + RoPE16 + LN Scale + resid_mix + Legal TTT. Key fixes: EMA reset on QAT activation eliminates quantization degradation; deterministic wallclock-fraction QAT trigger (LATE_QAT_FRAC=0.65) removes seed-to-seed QAT timing variance. 12L INT4 fits in 16MB via INT4 bigram QAT saving ~370KB vs INT6. Best result: 8×H100 seed 3, ttt_bpb=1.1594 (full 1893-chunk TTT). Seed 1: post-quant 1.1703, ttt_bpb~1.165.

submission.author: Roberto Arce
submission.name: 11L Partial RoPE + XSA4 + VE128 + Tight SWA + Late QAT + GPTQ-lite
submission.blurb: 11-layer 512-dim model with MLP hidden=1408 (2.75x). Partial RoPE (16/64 dims) leaves 75% of head dims position-free. LN Scale (1/sqrt(layer+1)) damps deeper layers. XSA on last 4 layers removes self-value bias via GQA-aware orthogonal projection. Shared Value Embedding (dim=128) on layers 9,10. SmearGate for bigram context. Tight SWA (scale<0.2, 6 checkpoints) for zero-penalty weight averaging. Late QAT (STE int6 at lr_scale<0.1) avoids Muon momentum corruption. GPTQ-lite per-tensor clip ratio search minimizes reconstruction error at quantization time. Int6 step=4 on layers 1-9, int8 on layers 0,10. FP16 tied embeddings. zstd-22 compression. Muon lr=0.025, momentum=0.99, WD=0.04. Batch=786K, seq=2048, warmdown=3000, grad_clip=0.3.

pr.title: Non-record: 11L Partial RoPE + XSA4 + VE128 + Tight SWA + GPTQ-lite (val_bpb=1.1804)

submission.blurb: Proxy-scale submission (GH200 MIG 3g.48gb, 600s). 5-layer 512-dim GQA+XSA transformer with MLP×4, Bigram-4096+1024dim bypass layer, EMA=0.97, Optuna v1 TPE optimizer tuning (25 trials). val_bpb=1.3246 (float, post-EMA) / 1.3069 (int6+zlib, stride=64). Not a SOTA record — proxy hardware. Requesting H100 access for full competition run.

pr.title: [Non-Record] 5L MLP×4 EMA=0.97 Optuna — GH200 proxy, val_bpb=1.3069 (int6+zlib)

submission.author: Tommy Markstein
submission.name: 8L EMA + Int6-MLP + BigramHash-65k + Sliding-64
submission.blurb: Full stack improvement across 41 experiments on the autoresearch/pgolf_mar22 branch. Key gains: (1) 8L architecture +0.007 bpb, (2) bigram_vocab 4096→65536 +0.015 bpb total, (3) eval_stride=64 free +0.007 bpb, (4) EMA decay=0.9 replacing uniform SWA +0.006 bpb, (5) warmdown extended to 3000 steps (~79% of training) +0.008 bpb, (6) int6 quantization for MLP (was int5) +0.0015 bpb, (7) 1% magnitude pruning before quantization +0.0009 bpb. Total improvement from session-2 baseline: 6.3% (1.3978→1.3092 bpb). Combined with session-1 gains: 26.5% from original 1.7810 baseline.

pr.title: Submission Record Series: BatchOpt+MLP4+RoPE100k and 8L EMA Int6 Bigram65k on Single 20GB GPU (val_bpb 1.7810 → 1.3092)

submission.author: jzgdev

Latest 8xH100 reference run reached 1.31011907 val_bpb at 14,545,165 bytes total, but this record remains exploratory pending proper multi-seed baseline-vs-PIC-GD evidence.

submission.author: Vilhelm Toivonen
submission.name: Depth-Recurrent UT + Rank-1 LoRA Per-Iteration Adaptation
submission.blurb: Universal Transformer (1 prelude + 4 shared x 3 loops + 1 coda = 14 effective layers from 6 unique blocks) at 640d with rank-1 LoRA per-iteration adaptation. Each loop iteration gets a unique weight modification via an outer product of two learned vectors stored on AdamW. This is the first stable per-iteration adaptation for recurrent transformers — rank-8 LoRA diverges due to Muon NS5 scale factor asymmetry on rectangular matrices. Output-LN, Birkhoff mixing, timestep scaling, noisy QAT. Artifact 11.3 MB.

pr.title: Record: Depth-Recurrent UT + Rank-1 LoRA Per-Iteration Adaptation — val_bpb 1.3342

submission.author: Daniel Nobrega Medeiros
submission.name: JEPA-LM: Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture for Text Compression
submission.blurb: First JEPA for text compression. Reinterprets U-Net transformer as encoder-decoder with 256-dim latent space, next-position predictor (MSE), and SIGReg anti-collapse regularizer. Loss: CE + 0.1*JEPA_MSE + 0.03*SIGReg. LeakyReLU(0.5)^2 activation. 19M params, 10L/512d. Novel architecture contribution - JEPA components add only ~131K params with zero eval overhead.

pr.title: Parameter golf jepa

submission.author: Gustav Welinder
submission.name: Stacked Hyperparameter Tuning + Eval2048
submission.blurb: Non-record (RTX 5090, 20 train shards): Stacked 5 hyperparameter fixes on the baseline 9x512 architecture. Key finding: WARMDOWN_ITERS=1200 is broken at 600s wallclock (fires from step 1); fix to 3000 plus MATRIX_LR=0.06, LOGIT_SOFTCAP=15, MUON_MOMENTUM=0.99, quarter-batch 131K tokens, and EVAL_SEQ_LEN=2048 together bring val_bpb from 1.362 to 1.336. Also tested butterfly MLP factorization, reservoir random MLPs, depth recurrence, and 6 alternative iso-byte shapes — none beat the tuned 9x512 baseline. 40+ total experiments via autonomous autoresearch loop.

pr.title: Non-record: Stacked hyperparameter tuning + eval2048 (RTX 5090, val_bpb 1.336)
Non-record: 10L MLP3x + Muon | val_bpb=1.3365 | Single Colab GPU

submission.author: Roland Sharp
submission.name: Causal Oscillator LM
submission.blurb: Physics-native language model using damped harmonic oscillator impulse responses for causal temporal encoding. No transformer blocks in the traditional sense — the transfer function H(ω) = 1/(ω₀² - ω² + 2iγω) creates temporal context through resonance.

pr.title: Causal Oscillator LM: physics-native architecture (BPB 1.34)

submission.author: Sidhant Thole
submission.name: Sequence-level V-shaped Curriculum + LeakyReLU² + AWQ + Cyclic Momentum + SWA
submission.blurb: Online sequence-level curriculum learning: oversamples 2× sequences per batch, scores each by unigram entropy H(s) = -Σ p(t)·log₂(p(t)), then selects the half matching a V-shaped difficulty target d(step) that ramps easy→hard→easy aligned with LR warmdown and SWA. Unlike shard-level curriculum (which fails because we only consume ~6 of 80 shards), this operates within each batch where meaningful variance exists. Combined with LeakyReLU(0.5)² activation, cyclic momentum (0.85–0.95), and SWA. Run on 1×H100 due to compute constraints. Built upon PR #623.

pr.title: [Non Record] Online Curriculum Learning

submission.author: wfproc
submission.name: QAT Dead-Code Analysis + Novel Technique Sweep (1xH100)
submission.blurb: Non-record research submission. Found that torch.compile constant-folds Late QAT in #315-derived code (confirmed in SOTA #549). Tested a fix via tensor-scale STE, but working QAT actually made the int6 gap worse. Also swept 7 untried techniques from recent papers (Muon-VS, Deep Delta Learning, Thinking Deeper recurrence, anti-layer removal, NS step reduction, spectral SVD compression, wider models). All negative on 1xH100. Key finding: at 83ms/step, each 1ms of overhead costs about 0.007 bpb.

submission.name: Text Diffusion: Masked Diffusion Language Model (MDLM)
submission.blurb: First text diffusion submission. MDLM training (Sahoo et al., NeurIPS 2024): random masking at rate t~U(0,1), predict masked tokens, ELBO-weighted loss. SUBS parameterization (carry-over unmasking, zero mask probabilities). Stratified t sampling. Causal attention for AR eval compatibility. Eval: sliding window.

pr.title: Notable Non-Record: Text Diffusion (MDLM) — 1.4584 BPB — Masked Diffusion Language Model

submission.author: Alfaxad
submission.name: BigramHash(4096) + Cosine EMA + Earlier QAT + LZMA-9
submission.blurb: Non-record submission (1xH100 only, 940 steps). Built on PR #549 stack with: BigramHash expanded 2048->4096, cosine EMA schedule (0.99->0.999), earlier late QAT (threshold 0.15->0.10), LZMA preset 6->9. Artifact 7.9MB (room for larger model). Developed using ShinkaEvolve (Sakana AI) evolutionary code optimization framework with GPT-5.4 and Gemini 3 Pro as mutation operators. 8xH100 evaluation pending.

pr.title: Non-record: BigramHash(4096) + Cosine EMA + LZMA-9

submission.author: Claude Haiku Autonomous Research
submission.name: Batch Optimization + MLP4 + RoPE100k
submission.blurb: Optimized training through: (1) batch size reduction 196k→98k enabling more steps within 600s window (+13.7%), (2) MLP multiplier increase 2→4 for wider FFN layers (+0.47%), (3) learning rate tuning matrix/scalar 0.04→0.035 (-0.32%), (4) warmdown schedule optimization 800→600 iters (+0.31%), (5) RoPE base adjustment 10k→100k for better positional encoding (+0.13%). Total improvement: 16.9% from 1.781→1.478 bpb.

pr.title: Submission Record Series: BatchOpt+MLP4+RoPE100k and 8L EMA Int6 Bigram65k on Single 20GB GPU (val_bpb 1.7810 → 1.3092)

submission.blurb: First non-Euclidean, Lorentzian geometry transformer in the competition. Replaces dot-product attention with Minkowski inner product on the hyperboloid manifold, using a custom Flash Lorentz Attention Triton kernel. Tri-block architecture with 8 curvature-progressive orbits through 3 shared blocks (23.9M stored, 62M effective params). d=768, 12H GQA 6:1, 5x MLP with fused LeakyReLU(0.5)². Parallel eval: 8 GPUs each run a different TTT hyperparameter, best reported. 1-seed: 1.5918.

submission.author: abaybektursun
submission.name: The N-gram BPB Scores Are Not Real
submission.blurb: N-gram caching claims sub-0.5 BPB but the scores come from an invalid probability distribution (sums to ~410, not 1). The hash ratio P(cache_bin) is not a conditional probability. Bucket sweep confirms: collision-free tables give baseline-level BPB. Proposes distribution verification and causality enforcement.

submission.name: Depth Recurrence 4x8 + LoRA + U-Net Skips + Layer Scale
submission.blurb: Replaces 9 unique transformer layers with 4 shared blocks looped 8 times (32 effective layers). Per-iteration rank-2 LoRA adapters on attention output projection differentiate behavior across iterations. U-Net skip connections pair encoder iterations (0-3) with decoder iterations (4-7). Per-iteration layer scale (init 1/8) prevents activation explosion. Gradient checkpointing for memory efficiency. Tighter grad clipping (0.1), 2000-step LR warmup, 2500-step momentum warmup. All other techniques preserved: int6+zstd-22 quantization, 3x MLP, SmearGate, BigramHash, orthogonal init, Muon WD=0.04, SWA every 50 steps.

submission.author: Mohamed Diomande
submission.name: Anticipatory Transformer: Entropy-Weighted Loss + Trajectory-Scaled Attention
submission.blurb: Builds on SOTA stack (10L, Int5-MLP/Int6-Attn, BigramHash 10240, SmearGate, OrthoInit, MuonWD, SWA, sliding window eval, zstd-22) with two parameter-free innovations from anticipation geometry: (1) entropy-weighted training loss that upweights hard tokens (alpha=0.15), focusing gradient signal on informative positions; (2) trajectory-scaled attention that computes a per-position commitment signal from hidden state velocity, amplifying stable representations. Both are zero-parameter, zero-compression-cost additions that improve training dynamics without changing the model size or architecture.

submission.author: Silas Alberti
submission.name: 11L WD0.038 Non-Val + Int6 + Sliding Window
submission.blurb: 11-layer GPT with GQA (NUM_KV_HEADS=4), decoupled weight decay (0.038) on Muon optimizer, int6 per-row quantization on MLP+attention weights, fp16 embeddings, zstd-22 compression, and sliding window evaluation (stride=64). Trained on standard training shards only (no val-only). Key innovations: weight decay reduces quantization gap and enables fitting 11 layers under 16MB; higher LR (0.025) improves non-val convergence.

submission.author: mrdavtan
submission.name: Non-record: SWA + Doc-Isolated Eval ablation (two negative findings)
submission.blurb: Three controlled runs on fast hardware (44ms/step) testing SWA and doc-isolated eval independently. SWA does not improve int8 quantization under default warmdown (delta +0.0004 BPB, within noise). Doc-isolated eval hurts by 0.009 BPB at stride=64, contradicting the LoRA TTT finding at stride=256. The crossover depends on stride length: at stride=64 context quantity dominates, at stride=256+ context quality dominates.

pr.title: Non-record: SWA and doc-isolated eval ablation — two negative findings at stride=64

submission.author: devin-ai
submission.name: 11L Int5-All + LN Scale + Partial RoPE + XSA6 + Adaptive Prune + Causal TTT (60 epochs, 64 chunks, LR=0.010)
submission.blurb: 11 layers with int5 quantization for all weight categories + other. LN Scale (1/sqrt(layer+1)). Partial RoPE (12 of 64 dims). EMA (decay=0.997) for model averaging. Adaptive magnitude pruning to guarantee under-16MB. Exclusive Self-Attention on last 6 layers. Legal causal test-time training: chunk-based eval-then-train on validation data (64 chunks, 60 SGD epochs per chunk, LR=0.010). Muon WD=0.08. SmearGate + OrthoInit + zstd-22.

submission.author: Xiaoan Liu
submission.name: Meta-Learned TTT + Error-Guided Adaptation Analysis
submission.blurb: Research submission exploring Reptile meta-learning for test-time training initialization and error-guided adaptation strategies under 16MB compression. Key findings: (1) Reptile meta-learning improves SmearGate models by 0.011 BPB (10x naive TTT), (2) Error-guided TTT is a negative result — concentrating adaptation on high-loss tokens does not help, (3) 13-layer models beat 10-layer on 8xH100 despite fewer training steps.

submission.author: Daniel Wahnich
submission.name: Aweb BitNet 1.58-bit Moonshot
submission.blurb: 1.58-bit ternary weights {-1,0,+1} with 2-bit packing: ~50M params in 16MB (vs baseline 17M). 6 unique blocks × 8 repeats = 48 effective depth at 1024 dim. Stacks 7 techniques: BitNet + Depth Recurrence + DiffAttn + SwiGLU + MoE (4 experts) + QAT (native) + TTT. Absmean quantization with straight-through estimator. 3× more depth, 2× wider, 3× more parameters than any competitor.

submission.author: Daniel Wahnich
submission.name: Aweb Depth Recurrence + MoE + TTT
submission.blurb: 4 unique blocks × 6 repeats = 24 effective layers at 768 dim with 4-expert MoE routing, SwiGLU activation, Quantization-Aware Training, and Test-Time Training at eval. Weight sharing gives 2.67× depth of baseline with same param budget. MoE specializes 4 tiny experts per block (192 hidden each). QAT trains through int8 noise. TTT adapts MLP weights on validation context before final scoring.

pr.title: 1.1190 BPB — 11L LeakyReLU² XSA4 PartialRoPE LNScale EMA ParallelMuon TTT
